content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the milestone lifecycle/journal changes in the milestone service, filesystem adapter, and run engine. Targeted `cargo test milestone_record::service` passes, but manual repros found two correctness bugs: paused milestones never become completed when the final bead finishes after a pause, and resume transitions are timestamped with the original run start, which also inflates `duration_seconds` metadata.

## Amendments

1. Fix paused-to-completed reconciliation in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:907-915`. The `snapshot.status == MilestoneStatus::Paused` branch currently runs before the all-beads-closed branch, so when the last running bead finishes after a pause, `reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage` preserves `paused` instead of promoting the milestone to `completed`. As a result, `update_task_run` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1947-1978`) writes only a `BeadCompleted` event and never emits the required lifecycle completion transition.
   Rationale: I reproduced this with a one-bead milestone: start -> pause -> complete returns `Ok(())`, but the snapshot stays `Paused` with progress `1/1` and the journal has no `StatusChanged(... -> completed)` entry. That violates the lifecycle contract for completed milestones.
2. Use the actual resume time for `paused -> running` lifecycle events. The resume path calls `sync_milestone_bead_start(..., resume_state.started_at)` in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1648-1653`, and `record_bead_start` then forwards `started_entry.started_at` into `build_reconciled_transition_events` at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1798-1805`. `build_reconciled_transition_events` uses that value as the running transition timestamp at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:739-748`, so resumed events are backdated to the original run start instead of the resume moment.
   Rationale: I reproduced this with a run that started at `10:00:01`, paused at `10:00:02`, resumed at `10:00:20`, and completed at `10:00:30`. The journal recorded `execution resumed` at `10:00:01`, and the completed transition reported `duration_seconds=30` instead of the correct 11 seconds of active runtime.
